req.title: Recurring transfer (boundary variant)
req.statement: A premium-tier customer shall be able to schedule a recurring transfer up to $10,000 per occurrence; non-premium accounts capped at $2,500. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-030 defines two equivalence partitions by tier (standard/premium), four boundary points ($1, $2500, $2501, $10000), and two error pathways (limit exceeded, unsupported frequency). The suite covers: (1) EP positive — premium tier schedules a mid-range transfer well within limits; (2) EP/DT negative — standard tier submits amount exceeding its $2500 cap, expecting 422; (3) BVA boundary — standard tier submits exactly $2500 (max allowed, inclusive); (4) BVA boundary — premium tier submits exactly $10000 (max allowed, inclusive); (5) NEG — unsupported frequency yields 422; (6) BVA boundary — standard tier submits $2501 (one above cap), expecting 422. Together these achieve boundary coverage at all threshold edges, decision-table coverage across tier×amount combinations, and negative path coverage for both error pathways cited in the requirement.

tests.name: test_premium_schedule_recurring_transfer_within_limit
actions: Authenticate as premium customer and obtain bearer token
POST /transfers/recurring with amount=5000, frequency=WEEKLY, start_date=next Monday
trace: R-FIN-030: premium tier cap is $10000; $5000 is a valid mid-partition value
R-FIN-030: schedule persisted and next run calculated on success
tests.executable:
def test_premium_schedule_recurring_transfer_within_limit(premium_auth_token, api_base_url, next_monday):
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "start_date": next_monday,
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = <http_client>.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {premium_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body, "schedule_id missing from response"
    assert "next_run" in body, "next_run missing from response"

tests.name: test_standard_schedule_recurring_transfer_exceeds_cap_returns_422
actions: Authenticate as standard customer and obtain bearer token
POST /transfers/recurring with amount=3000 (above $2500 standard cap), frequency=WEEKLY
expected: Response body contains error code indicating per-occurrence limit exceeded
trace: R-FIN-030: non-premium cap is $2500; $3000 is in the invalid partition
R-FIN-030: error pathway — 422 when per-occurrence limit exceeded
tests.executable:
def test_standard_schedule_recurring_transfer_exceeds_cap_returns_422(standard_auth_token, api_base_url, next_monday):
    payload = {
        "amount": 3000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "start_date": next_monday,
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = <http_client>.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {standard_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body, "error field missing"
    assert "limit" in body["error"].lower() or "exceeded" in body["error"].lower()

tests.name: test_standard_schedule_recurring_transfer_at_exact_cap_2500
actions: Authenticate as standard customer and obtain bearer token
POST /transfers/recurring with amount=2500 (exact cap boundary), frequency=WEEKLY
trace: R-FIN-030: $2500 is the maximum inclusive boundary for standard tier
R-FIN-030: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold must be tested
tests.executable:
def test_standard_schedule_recurring_transfer_at_exact_cap_2500(standard_auth_token, api_base_url, next_monday):
    payload = {
        "amount": 2500,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "start_date": next_monday,
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = <http_client>.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {standard_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body
    assert "next_run" in body

tests.name: test_standard_schedule_recurring_transfer_one_above_cap_2501_returns_422
actions: Authenticate as standard customer and obtain bearer token
POST /transfers/recurring with amount=2501 (one cent/unit above cap), frequency=WEEKLY
expected: Response body contains error indicating limit exceeded
trace: R-FIN-030: $2501 is the first value above the standard tier cap — upper boundary + 1
R-FIN-030: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold must be tested (off-by-one)

tests.name: test_premium_schedule_recurring_transfer_at_exact_cap_10000
actions: Authenticate as premium customer and obtain bearer token
POST /transfers/recurring with amount=10000 (exact premium cap), frequency=MONTHLY
trace: R-FIN-030: $10000 is the maximum inclusive boundary for premium tier
R-FIN-030: boundary behaviour at exact max threshold must be tested
tests.executable:
def test_premium_schedule_recurring_transfer_at_exact_cap_10000(premium_auth_token, api_base_url, next_monday):
    payload = {
        "amount": 10000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "start_date": next_monday,
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = <http_client>.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {premium_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201 at premium boundary, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in body
    assert "next_run" in body

tests.name: test_unsupported_frequency_returns_422
actions: Authenticate as premium customer and obtain bearer token
expected: Response body contains error indicating unsupported frequency
trace: R-FIN-030: error pathway — 422 when frequency unsupported
R-FIN-030: frequency=HOURLY is outside the accepted enumeration
tests.executable:
def test_unsupported_frequency_returns_422(premium_auth_token, api_base_url, next_monday):
    payload = {
        "amount": 500,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "HOURLY",
        "start_date": next_monday,
        "destination_account": "<dest_account_id>"
    }
    resp = <http_client>.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {premium_auth_token}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422 for unsupported frequency, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body
    assert "frequency" in body["error"].lower() or "unsupported" in body["error"].lower()

tests.name: test_tier_cap_decision_table_standard_vs_premium_same_amount
preconditions: Two customer accounts exist: one tier=standard, one tier=premium
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with amount=5000 as standard customer — expect 422
POST /transfers/recurring with amount=5000 as premium customer — expect 201
expected: Premium tier POST returns HTTP 201 with schedule_id and next_run
trace: R-FIN-030: same amount maps to different outcomes depending on tier — decision table condition coverage
R-FIN-030: standard cap $2500, premium cap $10000; $5000 crosses standard but not premium
